title: epipactis xiphophylla
intro: Epipactis xiphophylla, also known as the yellow-hooded helleborine, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the mountainous regions of China and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and relieving digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Epipactis xiphophylla is often used in tea infusions or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
